questions.question: What does the selector property in @Component define?
options: The CSS selector that styles the component
The HTML tag name used to render this component in templates
The route path that navigates to this component
The ID of the DOM element Angular mounts the component into

questions.question: What is the difference between property binding [src]='url' and interpolation {{ url }}?
options: They are identical — both set the src attribute to the url value
[src]='url' binds a TypeScript expression to a DOM property; {{ url }} renders a string in text content
Interpolation is faster; property binding requires extra compilation
[src]='url' only works in Angular 17+; {{ url }} works in all versions

questions.question: A standalone component uses CommonModule directives (*ngFor, *ngIf). Where must CommonModule be added?
options: In the AppModule's imports array
In the component's imports array inside @Component
As a parameter to the @Component decorator's providers array
It's included automatically — no import needed

questions.question: In Angular 17+, what is the syntax to conditionally render content using the new control flow?
options: *ngIf='condition'
[hidden]='!condition'
@if (condition) { ... }
v-if='condition'

questions.question: A component has isLoading = true. How do you conditionally add the CSS class 'loading' to a div?
options: <div class='{{ isLoading ? "loading" : "" }}'>
<div [class]='isLoading'>
<div [class.loading]='isLoading'>
<div ngClass='loading: isLoading'>
